mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor demand and impending earnings revisions, causing immediate risk-off positioning that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and industrial production indicates synchronized demand and supply expansion, confirming economic resilience that supports corporate profit margins and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative Rank IC when GDPC1 and INDPRO diverge, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when both series exceed their 12-month moving averages.

mechanism_narrative: During aggressive monetary tightening, elevated policy rates compress equity multiples and increase borrowing costs, causing the S&P 500 to decline over the subsequent 60 trading days as discount rates outpace earnings growth.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in > 55% of observations during tightening episodes, or the coefficient on FEDFUNDS in a multivariate regression becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When consumer credit outstanding exceeds its historical trend by a significant margin, it signals peak household leverage and impending consumption slowdown, which reduces corporate revenue growth and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are not significantly negative when RSAFS breaches the threshold, or the signal's predictive power vanishes after controlling for CPIAUCSL in a multivariate model.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls confirms robust labor market conditions, supporting consumer spending expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal's directional accuracy falls below 50% over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistically significant difference between positive and negative PAYEMS growth months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in housing starts and industrial production reflect broad-based economic acceleration, reducing recession probabilities and supporting equity risk premiums, which yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when both series rise, or the interaction term in a regression model is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) over a 5-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-pandemic supply normalization regime, a sustained decline in industrial production indicates weakening manufacturing demand and inventory destocking, which compresses corporate margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive when INDPRO breaches the threshold, or the signal's Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the opposite direction during the specified regime.
